headline: TRY is lagging peers, while incomplete domestic context limits the macro interpretation of the experimental price-history signal.

briefing.caveat_message: Fresh CBRT policy-rate and multi-week reserve evidence was unavailable. The price-history model can still run, but its domestic macro context is incomplete.
briefing.house_call: TRY is lagging peers, while incomplete domestic context limits the macro interpretation of the experimental price-history signal.

summary.primary_message: TRY is lagging peers, while incomplete domestic context limits the macro interpretation of the experimental price-history signal.
summary.market_message: USD/TRY is +1.56% over 20 sessions, with TRY vs peers at +0.03%.
summary.macro_message: Broad dollar change is +1.09%; a valid multi-week CBRT reserve trend was unavailable in this snapshot.
summary.news_message: Google News: 4 items, social chatter: unavailable items over the last 14 days; unavailable feeds: social chatter.

trigger_cards.detail: If TRY keeps lagging the peer basket beyond the current +0.03% gap, the read should move more bearish.

trigger_cards.detail: A fresh multi-week CBRT reserve series is required before reserve momentum can be described as supportive or deteriorating.

trigger_cards.title: Volatility regime
trigger_cards.detail: If global and EM volatility break higher together, shorter-horizon TRY risk should rise quickly.

history_entry.headline: TRY is lagging peers, while incomplete domestic context limits the macro interpretation of the experimental price-history signal.

model.name: Empirical regime-conditioned USD/TRY model
model.method: Expanding-window historical event rate conditioned on as-of 5-session momentum, 20-session momentum, realized volatility and acceleration; Bayesian shrinkage to the as-of climatology; rolling reliability recalibration.
model.excluded_context: Contemporaneous macro, reserve, volatility and news inputs are contextual only and do not alter the probability until point-in-time histories with release timestamps are available.
model.training_protocol: For every historical forecast, training labels are admitted only after their complete target window ends. Calibration uses only earlier forecasts whose outcomes were observable at that time.
limitations: The reference rate is derived from ECB EUR/TRY and EUR/USD observations, not an executable quote.
Long-horizon backtest outcomes overlap even though evaluation forecasts are sampled no more often than monthly.
Moving-block intervals retain observed target-window dependence but describe sampling uncertainty, not structural breaks.
Backtest metrics are research evidence, not a guarantee of future performance.

baseline.source: ECB reference rates, USD/TRY derived as EUR/TRY divided by EUR/USD

target.variable: future derived ECB USD/TRY daily reference rate

event_definition.measurement: ECB reference-rate close to close over an exact count of trading observations
event_definition.statement: For each horizon, the event occurs when the future USD/TRY reference rate is greater than or equal to the baseline multiplied by one plus that horizon's threshold.
event_definition.baseline_rule: latest common-date ECB EUR/TRY and EUR/USD observation available at publication
event_definition.target_rule: observation exactly h ECB trading observations after baseline

metric_definitions.brier_score: mean squared probability error; lower is better
metric_definitions.log_loss: mean negative Bernoulli log likelihood; lower is better
metric_definitions.brier_skill_vs_climatology: 1 - model Brier / target-purged as-of climatology Brier; each benchmark probability is stored when its forecast is formed; positive is better
metric_definitions.calibration_error: weighted absolute forecast-vs-outcome gap across probability bins; lower is better

data_health.cache_policy: Only semantically valid, fresh responses replace last-good payloads. Cached data must independently pass the same validation and observation-age gate.
